title: Cannot initialize podman machine on Windows 11 running on Microsoft Surface Laptop 7th edition with Snapdragon(R) X 12-core X1E80100 @ 3.40 GHz (3.42 GHz)
body: ### Issue Description When trying to initialize the Podman machine in the configuration wizard of Podman Desktop or by running `podman machine init`, I get the following error `Error: Command execution failed with exit code 125 Command execution failed with exit code 125` I'm running on a brand new Microsoft Surface Laptop 7th edition with the following config Processor Snapdragon(R) X 12-core X1E80100 @ 3.40 GHz (3.42 GHz) Installed RAM 32,0 GB (31,6 GB usable) System type 64-bit operating system, ARM-based processor Windows 11 24H2 OS: windows/amd64 provider: wsl version: 5.6.0 ### Steps to reproduce the issue Run the Setup wizard with default settings. I get the same results if I try to run `podman machine init` in the command line. ### Describe the results you received Describe the results you received ### Describe the results you expected Describe the results you expected ### podman info output ```yaml Looking up Podman Machine image at quay.io/podman/machine-os:5.6 to create VM Getting image source signatures Copying blob sha256:249b88fe5903cc7e6e695fc154fcbdfd04bc90599ed5b2a5a237cc849c7f3438 Copying config sha256:44136fa355b3678a1146ad16f7e8649e94fb4fc21fe77e8310c060f61caaff8a Writing manifest to image destination 249b88fe5903cc7e6e695fc154fcbdfd04bc90599ed5b2a5a237cc849c7f3438 Importing operating system into WSL (this may take a few minutes on a new WSL install)... The operation completed successfully. wsl: Processing /etc/fstab with mount -a failed. wsl: Failed to mount C:\, see dmesg for more details. wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\Users\CedricPONTET\AppData\Local\Programs\Podman Desktop' wsl: Failed to translate 'c:\Program Files\RedHat\Podman' wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\Program Files\Microsoft SDKs\Azure\CLI2\wbin' wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\WINDOWS\system32' wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\WINDOWS' wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\WINDOWS\System32\Wbem' wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\WINDOWS\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\' wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\WINDOWS\System32\OpenSSH\' wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\ProgramData\chocolatey\bin' wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\Program Files\PowerShell\7\' wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\bin' wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\Program Files\Git\cmd' wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\Program Files\dotnet\' wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\Program Files\starship\bin\' wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\Program Files\GitHub CLI\' wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\Program Files\Amazon\AWSCLIV2\' wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\Program Files\RedHat\Podman\' wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\Users\CedricPONTET\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps' wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\Users\CedricPONTET\.dotnet\tools' wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\ProgramData\nvm' wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\nvm4w\nodejs' wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\Users\CedricPONTET\.bun\bin' <3>WSL (10 - Relay) ERROR: CreateProcessCommon:735: execvpe(/bin/bash) failed: Exec format error Error: package permissions restore of shadow-utils on guest OS failed: command C:\WINDOWS\system32\wsl.exe [wsl -u root -d podman-machine-default rpm --restore shadow-utils] failed: exit status 1 Command execution failed with exit code 125 Command execution failed with exit code 125 Getting image source signatures Copying blob wsl: Processing /etc/fstab with mount -a failed. wsl: Failed to mount C:\, see dmesg for more details. wsl: Failed to translate 'C:\Users\CedricPONTET\AppData\Local\Programs\Podman Desktop' wsl: Upstream Latest Release Yes ### Additional environment details Additional environment details ### Additional information Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: I've just tested to install version  5.5.2 and it works. So it seems the issue is with version 5.6.0

> System type 64-bit operating system, ARM-based processor
> Windows 11 24H2
> OS: windows/amd64

You installed the wrong podman version then. You must install the arm version, i.e.  podman-installer-windows-arm64.exe
https://github.com/containers/podman/releases

I used `podman-desktop-1.21.0-setup-arm64.exe` to install the desktop and let it install podman. Maybe there is an issue there and the ARM version of the desktop is actually setting up the amd64 version of Podman?

If podman-desktop installs the wrong arch you would need to create an issue on their repo https://github.com/podman-desktop/podman-desktop

The podman-5.6.1-setup.exe name is deprecated and will be removed eventually as one should be using the per arch names so the user knows what they download.

And just to be sure it works when you install the arm version?

I have the identical system configuration, and `podman machine init` completes successfully when using the [arm64 specific installer](https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/download/v5.6.1/podman-installer-windows-arm64.exe) (but I can reproduce the same problem when using the amd64 specific installer). 

Note that after `podman machine init` failed, I had to run `wsl  --unregister podman-machine-default` to clean up the failed-to-start WSL instance (`podman machine rm` and `podman machine reset` were not enough).

> (podman machine rm and podman machine reset were not enough)

Sounds like a real bug, I assume it should be able to get us back to a clean state.
@l0rd can you elaborate on the what was insufficient? do you have logs of a failure or anything ?

@Luap99 @baude I have created the [issue related to the failed clean-up](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27036), I can also work to fix it if you are ok with it.

@cpontet I am closing this issue as I think that we have identified the problem and it's tracked at https://github.com/podman-desktop/podman-desktop/issues/12467, but feel free to re-open it if you think otherwise.
